concepts.title: Types of International Trade
concepts.description: There are two types of international trade: Import and Export. Import refers to the purchase of goods and services from another country, while export refers to the sale of goods and services to another country.

concepts.title: Reasons for International Trade
concepts.description: International trade takes place due to the law of comparative cost advantage, economies of scale, and differences in climate and resources.

concepts.title: Forms of International Trade
concepts.description: The two main forms of international trade are: (i) Bilateral trade: trade between two countries, and (ii) Multilateral trade: trade among more than two countries.

concepts.title: Countries with large trade deficits
concepts.description: Mnemonics: 'MAD' Countries (MAD- Countries with large trade deficits): USA, UK, and Australia.

concepts.title: Differences between Import and Export
concepts.description: Students often get confused between import and export. To avoid this, remember that Import is 'In' and Export is 'Out'. Import refers to goods and services coming into the country, while export refers to goods and services going out of the country.

concepts.title: International Trade Indicators
concepts.description: Key indicators of international trade include: (i) Balance of Trade (BOT), (ii) Balance of Payments (BOP), (iii) Current Account Balance (CAB), and (iv) Trade Deficit/Trade Surplus.

concepts.title: Advantages of International Trade
concepts.description: International trade offers several benefits, including: (i) Specialization and increased efficiency, (ii) Increased competition, (iii) Access to new markets and resources, and (iv) Economic growth and development.

concepts.title: Disadvantages of International Trade
concepts.description: International trade also has its drawbacks, such as: (i) Dependence on foreign markets and resources, (ii) Fluctuations in exchange rates, (iii) Environmental degradation, and (iv) Cultural homogenization.
